commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=eafc465d48a616c7b52d7b07bae4bf0c0824b14d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates doctor session route-state scanning and tests so stale `agentRuntimeOverride` and `agentHarnessId` runtime pins can be cleared more selectively.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: the PR's new Anthropic tests inject an owner directly, but production owners come from plugin doctor contracts and current main only exports a Codex session-route owner. I did not run a mutating `doctor --fix` command in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Human review is needed because proof is mock-only, the PR has a protected maintainer label, and maintainers need to choose how to fold in the missing owner-contract work.

target.title: fix(doctor): clear stale runtime override pins
target.body:
## Summary
- Include legacy `agentRuntimeOverride` entries in doctor session route-state detection.
- Treat stale owner-owned runtime overrides like stale `agentHarnessId` pins, including routes where the owner provider remains configured but the configured runtime moved elsewhere.
- Track the exact pinned runtime fields matched during scan so repair clears only stale owner-owned pins and preserves unrelated runtime overrides.
- Add regression coverage for stale `agentRuntimeOverride`-only sessions, provider-still-configured runtime changes, and mixed owner/non-owner runtime pins.

Fixes #83098.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-session-state-providers.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts`
- `node node_modules/@typescript/native-preview/bin/tsgo.js --noEmit --pretty false -p tsconfig.json`
- `git diff --check -- src/commands/doctor-session-state-providers.ts src/commands/doctor-session-state-providers.test.ts`
- `/mnt/c/src/claws-hapi/.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --parallel-tests "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-session-state-providers.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts"`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: `doctor --fix` can now detect and clear stale session-level `agentRuntimeOverride` pins such as `claude-cli` when the configured route no longer uses that runtime.
Real environment tested: WSL-local OpenClaw source worktree on current `origin/main`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-session-state-providers.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts`; `node node_modules/@typescript/native-preview/bin/tsgo.js --noEmit --pretty false -p tsconfig.json`; `git diff --check -- src/commands/doctor-session-state-providers.ts src/commands/doctor-session-state-providers.test.ts`; autoreview command listed above.
Evidence after fix: Focused Vitest passed 2 files / 102 tests; full `tsgo` probe passed; diff check passed; autoreview reported `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`.
Observed result after fix: Regression tests create stale `agentRuntimeOverride: "claude-cli"` sessions, including a route that still uses the Anthropic provider but has moved to `pi`; the doctor scanner reports a pinned-runtime repair and `applySessionRouteStateRepair` removes only the matched stale runtime field while preserving unrelated runtime overrides.
What was not tested: A live gateway or message-channel `/status` flow; this proof exercises the doctor session-state scan and repair path directly.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
